vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry indicates P1-P4, but the visual evidence shows only two visible pot structures (one yellow, one black) and a complete absence of identifiable plant biomass.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (No plant visible in yellow pot)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No plant visible in black pot)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (White rabbit anchor present, but host plant Pothos is absent)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine remains, but the Pothos is entirely absent.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated and dark; no visible moisture or fungal activity.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Contains debris, specifically white fragments resembling crushed eggshells or mineral deposits.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, the biome has transitioned from a state of potential neglect to total biological vacancy. The white object (likely a bottle or container) present in earlier frames has been removed or shifted out of view, and the soil surface shows no signs of life or recovery.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is non-viable. All registered specimens are considered lost. The environment is currently a sterile collection of pots and soil.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white, irregular fragments on the soil surface of the black pot suggests a previous intervention (likely calcium supplementation or decorative debris) that has not resulted in plant growth.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a total systemic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Despite the presence of the white rabbit anchor, the Pothos and other registered species are missing. The soil is barren, and the lack of any green biomass across the sequence indicates that the plants have either been removed or have perished and decomposed entirely. The environment is currently devoid of living botanical specimens.
